adds.rationale: Broadcom is the second-largest beneficiary of AI buildout after NVDA via custom ASICs (Google TPU) and AI networking silicon, and the VMware acquisition closed in November 2023, adding recurring software revenue.

news_evidence.summary: Broadcom completes $69B VMware acquisition after China approval, reshaping the company into a hybrid semi + infrastructure software giant.
Broadcom FY23 Q4 results: AI-related revenue tripled YoY to ~$1.5B per quarter, projected to be ~25% of FY24 semi revenue driven by custom ASIC and AI networking demand from hyperscalers.
Broadcom shares jump on FY24 outlook citing AI accelerator and Ethernet networking ramp as primary growth drivers.

adds.rationale: Global X Uranium ETF gives diversified uranium miners exposure complementing CCJ; spot uranium broke above $80/lb in late 2023 (highest since 2008) on supply deficits, COP28 nuclear pledges, and restart momentum.

news_evidence.summary: Spot uranium price tops $80/lb for first time since 2008 amid persistent supply deficit and renewed reactor builds.
22 nations including US, UK, France, Japan, Canada pledge at COP28 to triple global nuclear capacity by 2050.
Cameco raises long-term contracting prices as utilities scramble to lock in fuel; Kazatomprom guides 2024 production below earlier targets, tightening supply further.

removes.rationale: Broad healthcare ETF lagged S&P meaningfully in 2023 (+2% vs +24%) and the demographics/GLP-1 theme is already captured more directly via LLY; freeing the slot for higher-conviction adds.

news_evidence.summary: Healthcare sector worst-performing S&P 500 sector in 2023 as biotech weakness and Medicare price negotiation overhang weighed on the broad index.
GLP-1 obesity drug enthusiasm (Lilly Zepbound FDA approval Nov-2023, Novo Wegovy) drove dispersion within healthcare, rewarding stock picks over the broad XLV.

removes.rationale: Defense exposure is already covered by NOC and the ITA aerospace-defense ETF; LMT underperformed defense peers in 2023 on F-35 sustainment cost concerns and slowing growth, making it the weakest of the three defense holdings.

news_evidence.summary: Lockheed Martin shares finish 2023 down ~5% versus NOC roughly flat and ITA up ~10%, lagging on F-35 program cost overruns and tepid FY24 guide.
Lockheed Q3-23 results: organic sales growth guidance trimmed; F-35 sustainment cost growth flagged by Pentagon auditors.

rationale_overall: Two adds and two removes hold the watchlist at the 24-ticker cap while sharpening exposure to two of the profile's named next-wave themes. AVGO deepens AI exposure beyond NVDA/AMD into custom ASIC + AI networking, the second-derivative beneficiary of hyperscaler capex. URA broadens nuclear exposure from CCJ+CEG+BWXT+SMR (utility/SMR-heavy) into diversified uranium miners, capturing the late-2023 spot uranium breakout and COP28 tripling pledge. Removing XLV (broad healthcare lagging, GLP-1 captured via LLY) and LMT (redundant with NOC+ITA, 2023 underperformer) frees the slots without losing thesis coverage.
